hero.title: Data Science Interview Questions
hero.description: From data wrangling to advanced neural networks and natural language processing. Use the practice mode to hide answers and test your knowledge.

beginner.q: What is the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?
beginner.a: Supervised learning involves training a model on labeled data to make predictions, whereas unsupervised learning involves training a model on unlabeled data to discover patterns or relationships.

beginner.q: What is the role of a data scientist in a company?
beginner.a: A data scientist is responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting complex data to gain insights and inform business decisions, often using machine learning, statistical modeling, and data visualization techniques.

beginner.q: What are the key steps in the data science workflow?
beginner.a: The key steps in the data science workflow include problem definition, data collection, data cleaning and preprocessing, exploratory data analysis, modeling, evaluation, and deployment.

intermediate.q: How do you handle missing values in a dataset?
intermediate.a: There are several strategies for handling missing values, including listwise deletion, pairwise deletion, mean/median imputation, regression imputation, and multiple imputation, each with its own strengths and weaknesses.

intermediate.q: What is the concept of overfitting in machine learning?
intermediate.a: Overfitting occurs when a model is too complex and fits the training data too closely, resulting in poor performance on new, unseen data. Techniques such as regularization, early stopping, and cross-validation can help prevent overfitting.

intermediate.q: What is the difference between a decision tree and a random forest?
intermediate.a: A decision tree is a single tree-based model, whereas a random forest is an ensemble model that combines the predictions of multiple decision trees, reducing overfitting and improving overall performance.

advanced.q: How do you implement a neural network from scratch?
advanced.a: Implementing a neural network from scratch involves defining the architecture, initializing weights and biases, implementing forward and backward propagation, and training the model using an optimization algorithm such as stochastic gradient descent.

advanced.q: What is the concept of transfer learning in deep learning?
advanced.a: Transfer learning involves using a pre-trained model as a starting point for a new task, fine-tuning the weights and biases to adapt to the new data and task, often resulting in faster training and improved performance.

advanced.q: How do you evaluate the performance of a machine learning model?
advanced.a: Evaluating the performance of a machine learning model involves using metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, mean squared error, and R-squared, as well as techniques such as cross-validation and bootstrapping to estimate performance on unseen data.
